records.ety: [F. loquence, L. eloquentia, fr. eloquens. See Eloquent.]

defs: Fluent, forcible, elegant, and persuasive speech in public; the power of expressing strong emotions in striking and appropriate language either spoken or written, thereby producing conviction or persuasion.
quotes: Eloquence is speaking out . . . out of the abundance of the heart.

defs: Fig.: Whatever produces the effect of moving and persuasive speech.
quotes: Silence that spoke and eloquence of eyes.
The hearts of men are their books; events are their tutors; great actions are their eloquence.

defs: That which is eloquently uttered or written.
quotes: O, let my books be then the eloquence And dumb presagers of my speaking breast.
